guardrails.note: These are search-derived review candidates, including explicit rejects. A collect recommendation still requires rights, version, component, safety, provenance, and source-disjoint review before any corpus acquisition or training use.

candidates.title: National Tree Climbing Guide

redundancy.note: Complements existing USFS tree-hazard and trail-rigging documents with climbing-specific anchor, friction-saver, rope-care, and system material.

candidates.page_scope: Approximately 80 pages; collect the full guide only after component review.

authority.note: Agency-authored occupational tree-climbing guidance.

candidates.recency_version_needs: Published April 2015; check for a successor and retain edition/date metadata.

rights_signals.license_or_status: Likely United States federal public-domain text under 17 USC 105; separately credited figures, standards, and components require review.
rights_signals.visible_evidence: Official fs.usda.gov PDF and federal publisher.
rights_signals.tos_robots_ai: No additional TOS, robots, or AI-reuse signal surfaced in the Exa search-only pass.

safety_caveats: Professional tree climbing is not evidence that any tree or branch is suitable for human bondage suspension.
Species, decay, load direction, dynamic loading, local rules, equipment ratings, and qualified inspection remain controlling.
Do not transfer occupational system limits directly to shibari.

recommended_action.rationale: Unusually dense primary material for tree assessment, anchors, friction systems, inspection, and rope care; perform successor/component audit first.

candidates.canonical_url_status: Exact search-discovered URL recorded; canonical publisher URL and version must be confirmed wherever recency_version_needs or page_scope says resolution is pending.

redundancy.note: Overlaps existing government rigging sources but adds systematic knots, splices, guying, tackle, and natural/man-made anchor coverage.

authority.note: Official Army publication; includes older doctrine inherited from FM 5-125.

candidates.recency_version_needs: July 2012 manual based substantially on 1995 content; verify current status and mark legacy procedures explicitly.

rights_signals.tos_robots_ai: No additional TOS, robots, or AI-reuse signal surfaced in the Exa search-only pass.

safety_caveats: Heavy-load and military rigging practices are not human-suspension instructions.
Old load tables and hardware practices require current-standard verification.
No knot or anchor should be represented as safe for a body merely because it appears in this manual.

recommended_action.rationale: Dense, inspectable primary source for rigging vocabulary and mechanics, subject to an explicit legacy/currentness audit.
